title: CLI errors carry no remediation hints as a class — only FGAP-106 asks for one error's hint
description: FGAP-106 files the missing remediation hint on one error (the unresolvable config chain). No filing requires that CLI/op errors name their sanctioned remediation generally — which op un-bricks, which flag previews, which ceremony heals. The class: the error surface tells the operator what failed but not the sanctioned way out, in a system whose whole design is that every recovery has a sanctioned op. Surfaced as a coverage hole by the critical-focus outline derivation (dimension F).

evidence.reference: Representative instance (FGAP-106): the throw names the stuck version but no remediation op; the pattern generalizes across the error surface with no filing covering it.

impact: Operators (human or agent) hitting a refusal must already know the recovery op; errors dead-end sessions that the design intends to be self-recoverable.
proposed_resolution: Requires determination: an error-surface audit enumerating refusal/throw sites with a sanctioned-recovery op, adding the hint per site or via a shared error-shaping layer (the cli render/formatAjvError precedent). Positioned with MILE-008.
